tsTroubleshooting.domains: (topic tsTroubleshooting) (topic hi-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic ut-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic sw-d) (topic ui-d) (topic oce-d) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c tsTroubleshooting oceTroubleshooting-c)
tsTroubleshooting.title: Message in ORS: “Warning: the ProCare and Warranty overviews are more than 2 days old”

abstract.p: Message in ORS: “Warning: the ProCare and Warranty overviews are more than 2 days old”.
On top of the active printheads view in ORS - Remote data - ProCare, a date is shown.
All ProCare and warranty information is based on information up to this date (date of the most recent datalog taken into account).
A warning is displayed if this date is older than 2 days. The Colorado 1650 printers usually send data daily.
ProCare and warranty data is processed and updated every day based on available datalogs in ORS.
75% of all Colorado 1650 systems have data available that is not older than 2 days. That is caused by the request frequency of once per day.
If no recent data is available, you can request it by hitting the 'i'-button.
New incoming data can be monitored by checking the transaction log on the Remote data tab:
Error messages like the one above are an indication that something with the printer is not correct (e.g. data corruption on the controller, unstable network, etc).
Also consult the Device information to see the last heartbeat. The heartbeat is usually not older than approximately 20 minutes.

note.p: The ProCare and warranty screens are updated once per day and this cannot be initiated by any manual action in ORS. So when new data is received in ORS, still it will take a day before the ProCare and warranty screens will contain the new information.
